title: agelanthus gilgii
intro: Agelanthus gilgii, also known as the poison bush, is a plant species native to South Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. Agelanthus gilgii is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be under the guidance of a medical professional.
